evidence: The majority of this demand is driven by the power needs and projected power needs of data centers to serve an increasingly digital economy and to support artificial intelligence.
Extending service to these customers necessitates significant capital expenditures, which in turn requires sufficient access to sources of capital.

The traditional electric operating companies are experiencing projected demand that significantly exceeds recent experience, creating the need for new power generating resources and transmission facilities.
The majority of this demand is driven by the power needs and projected power needs of data centers... to support artificial intelligence.
Use of generative artificial intelligence has also increased the frequency, scale, and sophistication of cyber attacks and increased the capability of less sophisticated actors.

The increased concentration of business within a single industry based on emerging technologies, and uncertainties on the actual capacity required to satisfy the projected new demands... creates risks.
As generative artificial intelligence continues to evolve, new technologies and increased computing power could cause these trends to continue or exacerbate.

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No dollar amounts, GPU counts, or quantified capex figures tied specifically to AI infrastructure appear in the excerpts; score reflects named infrastructure context (data centers, new generation/transmission) without quantification.

ai_narrative_concreteness: AI discussed primarily as a demand driver for utility load growth, not as an internally deployed technology; no internal AI use cases or deployment metrics disclosed in excerpts.
capex_mentions: Significant capital expenditures referenced qualitatively but no specific dollar amounts tied to AI/data center investment appear in the excerpts.
